message:
fix(io): handle EINTR across all streaming read loops + quiet build emitter

Concurrent agent hardened IO read loops against EINTR (interrupted system
calls) across the entire codebase. On Unix, signal delivery (SIGCHLD,
SIGWINCH, etc.) can interrupt read() mid-call returning ErrorKind::Interrupted.
Previously this would either silently truncate output or surface a spurious
error string.

Changes by file:

- extension_dispatcher.rs: Two read loops updated:
  1. Exec output reader (line ~2511): EINTR now retries via continue instead
     of propagating error string to caller
  2. Stream chunk reader (line ~2888): Split Ok(0)|Err(_) into separate arms
     so EINTR retries while other errors still break

- extensions.rs: dispatch_hostcall_exec_ref read loop (line ~21962):
  Same EINTR-retry pattern as extension_dispatcher

- extensions_js.rs: stdout and stderr pipe read loops in JS child process
  execution (lines ~14773, ~14793): EINTR continues instead of returning
  error tuple

- rpc.rs: run_bash_rpc stream reader (line ~3437): Same split of
  Ok(0)|Err(_) pattern to retry on EINTR

- build.rs: Emitter now calls .quiet() to suppress warning noise from
  offloaded builds that temporarily miss git objects

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
